objective: Close F-0032, F-0085, and F-0087 without product-code changes by independently proving that current production routes use transactional database-backed resource facts, durable generation-based atomic index switching, and random stable resource public identifiers. Record the remaining RC-06 findings as separate confirmed roots and resume at F-0069. Do not modify product source, tests, schema, dependencies, permissions, Provider behavior, mechanisms, or historical evidence.
blockedFiles: permission, entitlement, Provider, external service, secret/env or deployment
PR, force-push, non-canonical remote, Subagent, kernel/guard/smoke

authoritativeChainEvidence.F-0032: Production App Router handlers construct the default PostgreSQL repositories; the local JSON adapter is reachable only by explicit test/local dependency injection. Resource metadata, upload operations, knowledge-node links, generations, and chunks are persisted transactionally.
authoritativeChainEvidence.F-0085: resource_index_generation owns pending/ready/failed/superseded facts; rebuild requests retain a ready active generation, completion atomically inserts chunks and switches the active generation, failures retain stale citations, and retrieval joins only active ready generation facts.
authoritativeChainEvidence.F-0087: Production upload preparation allocates resource-${randomUUID()} independently from the full content hash used for deterministic object storage and replay fingerprinting; the production path does not silently replace resources by a truncated hash identifier.

residualDisposition.next: F-0069 knowledge-node subtree move and rename atomicity

resumeAnchor.task: JIT-freeze and TDD the knowledge-node subtree move and rename atomicity boundary
resumeAnchor.condition: do not combine unrelated RC-06 findings or change schema, dependencies, permissions, Provider behavior, mechanisms, or historical evidence
